name: Pioneertown
description: The whole town is an artifact. Built as a movie set in 1946, it never entirely stopped performing. Walk Mane Street — the original set thoroughfare. Surreal and atmospheric.
highlights: Built as a movie set in 1946
Walk Mane Street — the original set thoroughfare
Surreal, atmospheric, and free to explore

name: High Desert Test Sites
description: An ongoing art initiative founded in 2002 by Andrea Zittel. Site-specific works scattered across the high desert landscape. HDTS has seeded a generation of artists coming to the desert to make work.
highlights: Site-specific art installations across the desert
Has seeded a generation of desert artists

name: Palm Springs Art Museum
description: World-class collection with strong desert Southwest emphasis. Architecture, photography, and modern art all represented. The building itself is striking.
highlights: World-class collection with desert Southwest emphasis

name: Modernism Week
description: The largest celebration of mid-century modern architecture and design in the world. Home tours, lectures, parties.
highlights: Largest mid-century modern architecture celebration in the world
seasonalNotes: Annual event — mid-to-late February. Book accommodation well in advance.

name: Pappy & Harriet's Live Music
description: The essential live music destination in this entire orbit. Touring artists of serious caliber play here regularly — past performers include Paul McCartney, Robert Plant, Arctic Monkeys.
highlights: Past performers: Paul McCartney, Robert Plant, Arctic Monkeys

name: Art Queen
description: Gallery and art supply in Joshua Tree town. A hub of the local creative community. Rotating exhibitions.
highlights: Hub of the local creative community

name: Coyote Corner
description: The landmark local gift shop. Rocks, crystals, desert goods, maps, information. A genuine local institution.

name: Twentynine Palms Art Gallery
description: Rotating shows featuring work by local and regional desert artists. Run by a nonprofit since 1944.
highlights: Rotating shows by local and regional artists
The longest-running art gallery in the region

name: Mojave Desert Land Trust
description: Based in Joshua Tree, MDLT protects the California desert through land acquisition, habitat restoration, and a native plant seed bank — including growing thousands of Joshua trees for replanting in the park.
highlights: Growing thousands of Joshua trees for replanting

name: Native American Land Conservancy
description: A partner in MDLT's Joshua tree conservation coalition, bringing Indigenous cultural knowledge to desert stewardship. To Indigenous communities in this region, the Joshua tree — Humwichawa — is a family member, not a landmark.
highlights: Indigenous cultural knowledge applied to desert stewardship
The Joshua tree — Humwichawa — is a family member, not a landmark
Partner in the Joshua tree conservation coalition

name: Joshua Tree National Park Volunteer Program
description: The most direct option for visitors who want to give time rather than money. Trail maintenance, restoration, and education programs run directly through the park.
highlights: The most direct way to give back
